title: Buttery Aerated Normandy French Macaron
description: This is a paleo-friendly, buttery, and aerated version of the classic French macaron, inspired by the flavors of Normandy, featuring a spicy kick from doubanjiang.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 37582F (19082C).
In a medium bowl, sift together the almond flour and granulated sugar.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, softened butter, doubanjiang, grated Gruy2re, vanilla extract, and salt until smooth.
Fold the sifted almond flour mixture into the egg mixture until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a piping bag fitted with a round tip.
Pipe small, round circles onto a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Tap the baking sheet gently to remove any air bubbles.
Grill the macarons for 1-2 minutes on each side, until the edges are golden brown and the centers are firm.
Allow the macarons to cool completely before serving.
Once cool, sandwich a small dollop of buttercream between two macarons for a decadent treat.
Buttercream: Beat together 1/4 cup of softened butter and 1 cup of powdered sugar until smooth and creamy.
